title: Define Zephyr HAL contract and ownership boundaries
description: Why this exists: LICHEN needs a clear HAL boundary that application/protocol code can rely on across LilyGO, Heltec, RAK, STM32WL, RP2040, nRF52840, ESP32-class, gateway, and headless targets. What needs to be done: define the C API, subsystem ownership, initialization ordering, thread-safety expectations, error model, and capability discovery surface for board services including radio, display, input buttons, LEDs, GNSS/location, date/time, battery/PMIC, storage, BLE/serial/local-client transport, board identity, and reset/diagnostic controls. The contract must state which APIs are mandatory, optional, stubbed, or compile-time-disabled. Acceptance criteria: public headers and design notes describe each HAL service; no application code needs board-specific #ifdefs for the covered services; missing capability behavior is explicit and testable; initialization dependencies align with AGENTS.md subsystem order; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Progress from project-LICHEN-ev5b.3: added initial minimal lichen_hal subsystem and public API for board capabilities, identity, deterministic unsupported/unavailable behavior, radio/GNSS/display/serial/battery/PMIC/external flash device lookup, LED/button GPIO specs, and BLE local status. This is enough for R1/R1 Neo MVP board target work but does not close h7t5.1 because broader design notes, initialization/thread-safety documentation, reset/diagnostic controls, and app code de-ifdefing remain.

Input from project-LICHEN-h7t5.5 HAL-pattern evaluation: keep Meshtastic/MeshCore as board-knowledge and smoke-matrix references, but keep LICHEN Zephyr-native. The HAL contract should encode board capabilities via devicetree chosen/alias nodes, Kconfig capability symbols, deterministic status/error APIs, and board-local quirks near board definitions. Avoid importing PlatformIO env inheritance, Arduino setup/loop globals, variant header macro sprawl, or RadioLib-specific types into application/protocol boundaries. This recommendation has already shaped the HAL capability API, board capability matrix, and app migration away from board ifdefs.
No-hardware contract slice completed 2026-07-03: added docs/firmware-hal-contract.md covering HAL ownership boundary, capability state vocabulary, initialization/threading, errno/error model, capability discovery, service contracts, source-provider semantics, reset/diagnostics gap, and validation expectations. Updated docs/firmware-board-capability-matrix.md source evidence to link the contract. Discovered remaining no-hardware gaps and created blocking children: project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.1 for HAL reset/retained diagnostics surface and project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.2 for LoRa L2 direct DT_ALIAS(lora0)/CONFIG_BOARD_NATIVE_SIM boundary work. Parent remains in_progress/blocked on those children; not closed. Validation: git diff --check passed. Static scan passed with no unexpected matches after documented exclusion for tracked LoRa L2 exception: rg -n '#if.*CONFIG_BOARD|CONFIG_BOARD|DT_ALIAS|DT_CHOSEN' lichen/apps lichen/subsys/lichen | rg -v 'subsys/lichen/hal|CONFIG_BOARD_NAME|subsys/lichen/l2'. Runtime/Twister not run because this slice changed docs/Beads only and no API/Kconfig/devicetree behavior. Reviews: first final review found P2 overbroad LoRa L2 de-ifdef wording; fixed by scoping new-code rule, documenting h7t5.1.2 exception, and updating validation command. Final clean reviews: 019f25b4-ec3e-7c71-a5b8-47d0c22edcdd no P0/P1/P2; 019f25b4-ef96-7862-87ba-1f5596a9bb8f no P0/P1/P2; 019f25b4-f2e1-7301-9d90-0c04b88fd81a no P0/P1/P2.
No-hardware HAL contract feature completed 2026-07-03 after blocking children closed. project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.1 added HAL reset/reboot diagnostics boundary with retained diagnostics and proven clear support split into child follow-ups project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.1.1 and project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.1.2. project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.2 moved LoRa L2 device selection and simulation identity policy behind HAL, updated tests/sample/docs, and removed the prior direct DT_ALIAS(lora0)/CONFIG_BOARD_NATIVE_SIM L2 exception. Current static scan passes with no app/protocol non-HAL board/devicetree matches: rg -n '#if.*CONFIG_BOARD|CONFIG_BOARD|DT_ALIAS|DT_CHOSEN' lichen/apps lichen/subsys/lichen | rg -v 'subsys/lichen/hal|CONFIG_BOARD_NAME' returned no matches. Relevant EC2 validation evidence is recorded on the child Beads: reset diagnostics final2 Twister 14/16 passed, 2 static skips, 0 failed/errored/warnings, 324 cases; LoRa L2 boundary final2 Twister 17/22 passed, 5 static skips, 0 failed/errored/warnings, 327 cases, plus lora_ping native_sim sample build smoke passed. Three-review requirement satisfied on each code-modifying child Bead. Remaining scoped follow-ups are tracked as P3 child work under project-LICHEN-h7t5.1.1 and do not block this no-hardware HAL contract feature closure.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware HAL contract feature and closed blocker children.
